1→"""Helper functions for dispatch command.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 6→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 7→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 8→ 9→ 10→def ensure_trunk_synced(ctx: ErkContext, repo: RepoContext) -> None: 11→ """Ensure local trunk ref is synced with remote, without requiring checkout. 12→ 13→ Uses fetch + update_local_ref to advance the local trunk branch pointer 14→ without needing trunk to be checked out. If trunk IS checked out in a 15→ worktree, falls back to requiring a clean worktree before updating. 16→ 17→ Raises SystemExit(1) on sync failure with clear error message. 18→ """ 19→ trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root) 20→ 21→ # Fetch latest remote trunk 22→ ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", trunk) 23→ 24→ local_sha = ctx.git.branch.get_branch_head(repo.root, trunk) 25→ remote_sha = ctx.git.branch.get_branch_head(repo.root, f"origin/{trunk}") 26→ 27→ if local_sha == remote_sha: 28→ return # Already synced 29→ 30→ # Check if we can fast-forward (local is ancestor of remote)
... [45 lines omitted] ...

290→ issues = FakeGitHubIssues() 291→ 292→ # Compose github with issues 293→ # If github is provided without issues_gateway, use github as-is (it has its own issues) 294→ # Only inject issues if caller explicitly passed BOTH github and issues 295→ if github is None: 296→ github = FakeGitHub(issues_gateway=issues) 297→ elif isinstance(github, FakeGitHub) and issues_explicitly_passed: 298→ # Caller passed both github and issues separately - inject issues 299→ # into the existing FakeGitHub instance to preserve test references 300→ github._issues_gateway = issues 301→ 302→ if github_admin is None: 303→ github_admin = FakeGitHubAdmin() 304→ 305→ if plan_store is None: 306→ from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime 307→ 308→ plan_store = PlannedPRBackend(github, issues, time=FakeTime()) 309→ 310→ # Handle graphite based on global_config.use_graphite to match production behavior 311→ # When use_graphite=False, use GraphiteDisabled sentinel so that 312→ # ErkContext.branch_manager returns GitBranchManager 313→ graphite_branch_ops: GraphiteBranchOps | None = None 314→ if graphite is None: 315→ # Need to check global_config.use_graphite - but it might be None or not set yet 316→ # If global_config is None, default will be set later with use_graphite=False 317→ use_graphite_from_config = ( 318→ global_config.use_graphite if global_config is not None else False 319→ )

1→"""Centralized test environment helpers for simulating erk scenarios. 2→ 3→This module provides helpers for setting up realistic erk test environments 4→with Click's CliRunner. It provides two patterns: 5→ 6→1. erk_isolated_fs_env(): Filesystem-based (uses isolated_filesystem(), 7→ creates real directories) 8→2. erk_inmem_env(): In-memory (uses fakes only, no filesystem I/O) 9→ 10→Key Components: 11→ - ErkIsolatedFsEnv: Helper class for filesystem-based testing 12→ - ErkInMemEnv: Helper class for in-memory testing 13→ - erk_isolated_fs_env(): Context manager for filesystem-based tests 14→ - erk_inmem_env(): Context manager for in-memory tests 15→ 16→Usage Pattern: 17→ 18→ Before (raw isolated_filesystem pattern - 20-30 lines per test): 19→ ```python 20→ def test_something() -> None: 21→ runner = CliRunner() 22→ with runner.isolated_filesystem(): 23→ cwd = Path.cwd() 24→ git_dir = cwd / ".git" 25→ git_dir.mkdir() 26→ erk_root = cwd / "erks" 27→ erk_root.mkdir() 28→ 29→ git = FakeGit(git_common_dirs={cwd: git_dir}) 30→ global_config = GlobalConfig.test(...)

Custom values can be provided 400→ via keyword arguments. 401→ 402→ Args: 403→ current_branch: If provided, auto-configures FakeGit.current_branches 404→ trunk_branch: Default branch name (default: "main") 405→ use_graphite: Enable Graphite integration (default: False) 406→ git: Custom FakeGit instance (overrides smart defaults) 407→ graphite: Custom FakeGraphite instance 408→ github: Custom FakeGitHub instance 409→ shell: Custom FakeShell instance

1→"""Unit tests for repository discovery with GitHub identity extraction.""" 2→ 3→from pathlib import Path 4→ 5→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext, discover_repo_or_sentinel, in_erk_repo 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 7→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit 8→ 9→ 10→def test_discover_repo_extracts_github_identity_https(tmp_path: Path): 11→ """Test that GitHub identity is extracted from HTTPS remote URL.""" 12→ repo_root = tmp_path / "test-repo" 13→ erk_root = tmp_path / ".erk" 14→ 15→ # Configure FakeGit with a GitHub HTTPS remote 16→ git_ops = FakeGit( 17→ worktrees={repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=repo_root, branch="main", is_root=True)]}, 18→ git_common_dirs={repo_root: repo_root / ".git"}, 19→ existing_paths={repo_root, repo_root / ".git"}, 20→ remote_urls={(repo_root, "origin"): "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk.git"}, 21→ ) 22→ 23→ result = discover_repo_or_sentinel(repo_root, erk_root, git_ops) 24→ 25→ assert isinstance(result, RepoContext) 26→ assert result.github is not None 27→ assert result.github.owner == "dagster-io" 28→ assert result.github.repo == "erk" 29→ 30→

session.assistant:
Now let me create a comprehensive summary with all the information I've gathered:
## Summary: Building ErkContext and RepoContext for Testing ensure_trunk_synced
Based on my exploration of the test codebase, here's exactly how to build an `ErkContext` and `RepoContext` for testing functions like `ensure_trunk_synced(ctx: ErkContext, repo: RepoContext)`:
### Key Files and Pattern
**File Locations:** - Function signature: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_helpers.py` (line 10) - `ErkContext` definition: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py` (re-exported from erk_shared) - Test factory: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py` (line 195: `context_for_test()`) - Test environment helpers: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/env_helpers.py` - Context builders: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/context_builders.py` - Example test: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_dispatch.py` (lines 24-148)
### Pattern 1: Using env.build_context() (RECOMMENDED)
This is the preferred pattern for most tests:
```python from click.testing import CliRunner from erk.cli.cli import cli from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env
def test_ensure_trunk_synced_when_already_synced() -> None: """Test ensure_trunk_synced when local trunk is already synced.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env: # Create FakeGit with trunk branch setup git = FakeGit( git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, current_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, local_branches={env.cwd: ["main"]}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, remote_urls={(env.cwd, "origin"): "https://github.com/owner/repo.git"}, remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main"]}, )
# Build context with defaults (no CLI runner needed) ctx = env.build_context(git=git, current_branch="main")
# Now you have: # - ctx.git configured with FakeGit # - ctx.cwd set to env.cwd # - Default implementations for all other dependencies
# Get RepoContext from the environment repo = env._repo # or pass custom RepoContext
# Call your function directly from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo) ```
### Pattern 2: Using context_for_test() (For Full Control)
When you need explicit control over all parameters:
```python from pathlib import Path from erk.core.context import context_for_test, ErkContext from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite from erk_shared.context.types import GlobalConfig from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env
def test_ensure_trunk_synced_diverged() -> None: """Test ensure_trunk_synced when trunk has diverged from remote.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env: # Setup Git git = FakeGit( git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, current_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, local_branches={env.cwd: ["main"]}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, remote_urls={(env.cwd, "origin"): "https://github.com/owner/repo.git"}, remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main"]}, repository_roots={env.cwd: env.cwd}, )
# Setup GitHub github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True) issues = FakeGitHubIssues() graphite = FakeGraphite()
# Setup GlobalConfig global_config = GlobalConfig.test( erk_root=env.erk_root, use_graphite=False, )
# Create RepoContext (from the test environment) repo = RepoContext( root=env.cwd, repo_name="repo", repo_dir=env.erk_root / "repos" / "repo", worktrees_dir=env.erk_root / "repos" / "repo" / "worktrees", pool_json_path=env.erk_root / "repos" / "repo" / "pool.json", )
# Build ErkContext using context_for_test ctx: ErkContext = context_for_test( cwd=env.cwd, git=git, github=github, issues=issues, graphite=graphite, global_config=global_config, repo=repo, )
# Call your function from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo) ```
### Key Components Explained
**ErkContext:** Unified dependency injection container that includes: - `git`: FakeGit (git operations) - `github`: FakeGitHub (GitHub API calls) - `graphite`: FakeGraphite (Graphite stack operations) - `console`: FakeConsole (user I/O) - `shell`: FakeShell (subprocess simulation) - Many other integration points
**RepoContext:** Repository metadata container that includes: - `root`: Path to repo root - `repo_name`: Repository name - `repo_dir`: Erk metadata directory - `worktrees_dir`: Worktrees metadata - `pool_json_path`: Worker pool configuration
**erk_isolated_fs_env():** Context manager that provides: - `env.cwd`: Current working directory - `env.git_dir`: .git directory path - `env.erk_root`: Erk metadata root - `env.build_context()`: Helper to build ErkContext - `env._repo`: Default RepoContext
### Real Example from Test Suite
From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_dispatch.py` (lines 24-148):
```python def test_dispatch_planned_pr_plan_triggers_workflow_with_planned_pr_backend() -> None: """Test that dispatching a planned-PR plan triggers workflow.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env: plan_branch = "draft-pr-plan-branch"
# Setup fakes fake_gh = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, polled_run_id="12345") fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() fake_time = FakeTime()
planned_pr_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_gh, fake_issues, time=fake_time)
git = FakeGit( git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, current_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, local_branches={env.cwd: ["main"]}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, remote_urls={(env.cwd, "origin"): "https://github.com/test-owner/test-repo.git"}, remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main", f"origin/{plan_branch}"]}, repository_roots={env.cwd: env.cwd}, )
graphite = FakeGraphite(authenticated=True, branches={...})
# Build context ctx = build_workspace_test_context( env, git=git, graphite=graphite, github=fake_gh, issues=fake_issues, use_graphite=True, plan_store=planned_pr_backend, )
# Invoke command result = runner.invoke(cli, ["pr", "dispatch", "42", "--base", "main"], obj=ctx) ```
### Helper Functions in Test Utils
**`build_workspace_test_context()`** - From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`: ```python def build_workspace_test_context( env: ErkInMemEnv | ErkIsolatedFsEnv, *, dry_run: bool = False, use_graphite: bool = False, current_branch: str | None = None, **kwargs, ) -> ErkContext: ```
This is a convenience wrapper that wraps `env.build_context()` with: - Smart FakeGit defaults - Remote URL configuration - Dry-run wrapping if needed - Graphite integration toggle
### Critical Distinctions
1. **Two `context_for_test()` implementations:** - `erk-shared`: Uses `github_issues=` parameter - `src/erk`: Uses `issues=` parameter - Always use the `src/erk` version for CLI tests
2. **ErkContext vs Click Context:** - ErkContext: Dependency injection container - Click Context: CLI framework context - Tests inject ErkContext via `obj=ctx` to CliRunner
3. **RepoContext is separate:** - Not part of ErkContext - Passed as separate parameter to functions like `ensure_trunk_synced()` - Created via `RepoContext()` constructor or environment defaults
### When to Use Each Pattern
| Use Case | Pattern | Example | |----------|---------|---------| | Simple test, standard setup | `env.build_context()` | Most tests | | Custom git/graphite config | `env.build_context(git=..., graphite=...)` | Branch-specific tests | | Full control over all deps | `context_for_test(...)` | Complex multi-integration scenarios | | Direct function test (no CLI) | Either above | Testing utility functions | | CLI command test | `env.build_context()` + `runner.invoke()` | Command tests |
The key insight: **`env.build_context()` is the recommended pattern** because it handles all the boilerplate of FakeGit setup, RemoteURL configuration, and GlobalConfig defaults. Only drop to `context_for_test()` when you need explicit control over specific parameters.
